name: Same Threat Detected on Same Host (by Machine Identifier)
notes: This rule triggers when the same threat has been detected multiple times on the same host. This could indicate that the AV is cleaning a file generated by the threat and not the threat itself. The time window should be large enough to cover at least 2 cycles of checks made by the AV.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Endpoint Protection Devices, BB:DeviceDefinition: IDS / IPS
when at least 5 events are seen with the same Threat Name (custom), Machine Identifier (custom) in 1 hour(s)
thresholds: when at least 5 events are seen with the same Threat Name (custom), Machine Identifier (custom) in 1 hour(s)
TD_list: APPLY Same Threat Detected on Same Host (by Machine Identifier) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Endpoint Protection Devices, BB:DeviceDefinition: IDS / IPS
AND when at least 5 events are seen with the same Threat Name (custom), Machine Identifier (custom) in 1 hour(s)
RED_list: Event name: Same Threat Detected on Same Host (Machine ID)
Event description: Same threat has been detected on the same host.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Threat Name (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
